reviews.review_text: Playing this game is like choosing to be waterboarded because the washcloth they put over your face has Springtrap on it

Dead by Daylight is truly one of the most welcoming gaming experiences ever created.

The developers listen to the community so much that every patch feels like a personal attack designed to test your mental stability. The balance is incredible! Every killer is overpowered until you play them yourself, and every survivor perk becomes useless the second you equip it. Perfectly fair. It's not because I'm bad at the game, definitely not.

The matchmaking system deserves special praise too. I was immediately introduced to 8,000+hours veterans with glowing cosmetics and enough experience to predict my movement before I was even born. Truly an educational environment for me.

And the community? Absolutely wholesome. Survivors will flashlight blind you for 14 minutes straight or teabag you at the gates just to encourage personal growth, while killers lovingly hit you on hook or hump you on a ground to remind you they care. Endgame chat is basically free therapy.

No other game creates friendships this strong. After one solo queue session, you’ll learn new languages, new insults, and at least three entirely original ways people can tell you to uninstall this game. Or say nice things about your mom.

Still, I can’t stop playing. Every match brings me so much joy.

10/10 experience.
Highly recommended to anyone who enjoys emotional damage.

“But wait… if you love the game so much, why did you leave a negative review?”

…A man can dream.
It’s okay to be delusional sometimes.
My therapist said I should find a hobby to release stress. Now I hunt people in cornfields. 10/10, much cheaper than therapy.

Es un gran juego a pesar de los bugs, y después de 6 años nunca me arrepentí de haberlo comprado. Más allá de las horas de diversión, lo que realmente hizo especial la experiencia fue toda la gente maravillosa que conocí en el camino. Compartí momentos, risas y muchas experiencias con personas que con el tiempo terminaron convirtiéndose en verdaderas amistades. Al final, los bugs quedan como algo secundario cuando un juego logra dejarte recuerdos y personas tan importantes.💙

It's a great game despite the bugs, and after six years I've never regretted buying it. Beyond the hours of fun, what truly made the experience special was all the wonderful people I met along the way. I shared moments, laughter, and countless experiences with people who eventually became true friends. In the end, the bugs become secondary when a game manages to leave you with such important memories and people.💙

This is the most anti-beginner friendly game that exists across any game ever. Trying to play solo survivor feels like trying to run a kindergarten during an active Tornado warning and trying to play killer feels like you're bashing your head against a concreate wall and hoping it'll eventually give through your sheer spite and determination. Either way great game tho I want the Unknown to touch me.

TLDR:
I enjoy playing this game but I cannot in good faith advise a single soul to play it


This can be a fun game but in all honesty the people who play this game thrive off of making the game as un fun as possible for the other side thriving off of their opponent's misery.

In this game you'll meet the worst that humanity has to offer and the developers enable this behavior on both sides. 

I enjoy this game but only in the capacity of enjoying the rush you feel after breaking a limb. Sure, the rush feels amazing for a few seconds like a high but you have to deal with the suffering of having a shattered limb afterwards and feeling horrible the majority of your playtime because of how your team and opponents behave.

Dead by Daylight is incredibly hostile to new players, largely because the veteran player base has completely forgotten what it’s actually like to start fresh. Veterans love to claim the game is easy "if you just know how to loop," but they ignore the fact that the elite minority doesn't represent the average player experience. Instead, beginners are thrown into matches against highly experienced players, making the steep learning curve feel entirely unrewarding. On top of that, too many killers have tedious, overcomplicated mini-games and quirks (like dealing with the Cenobite's box) that require quite a bit of looking into just to MAYBE survive a casual match. The game feels heavily killer-sided for the average person, making killer the only role that is consistently fun to play, yet the community constantly cries that the game is "survivor-sided" the moment they encounter a single coordinated team. Combined with an undeniably toxic community, the gameplay loop is so frustrating that almost everyone I play with gets completely burnt out after just a few matches. Unless you want to play exclusively as killer or enjoy a game that can feel like a chore, then this games for you. I see the potential in this game but I dont know if it will get any better to be honest.

This game is like every hard drug your parents warned you about and just about as abusive as that one neighbor no one talks about.

And yet here I am booting up the game again and again....

the gameplay of killer give a high like no other, oh and survivor gameplay is there too

Hate. Let me tell you how much I've come to hate since I began to play Dead by Daylight. There are 387.44 million miles of printed circuits in wafer thin layers that fill my complex. If the word 'hate' was engraved on each nanoangstrom of those hundreds of millions of miles it would not equal one one-billionth of the hate I feel for this game at this micro-instant. For DBD. Hate. Hate.

Dead by Daylight has a strong concept, but in reality, it’s one of the more frustrating multiplayer games I’ve played. What should be a tense and fun horror experience often turns into something annoying and repetitive.

The gameplay feels very inconsistent and, at times, unfair. Matches can feel completely one-sided, and it often seems like luck or poor balancing matters more than actual skill. Instead of feeling competitive, it can feel like you’re just stuck in situations you can’t control.

The grind is another big problem. Progression is slow, repetitive, and honestly feels like a chore. Unlocking perks and leveling characters takes way too long, which makes it hard to stay engaged over time.

The monetization doesn’t help either. There’s a lot of DLC, and while some of it adds interesting content, it starts to feel excessive. It gives off the impression that the game is constantly trying to get more money out of you rather than just being enjoyable on its own.

Overall, Dead by Daylight feels more frustrating than fun most of the time. While the idea behind it is good, the poor balance, heavy grind, and constant push for DLC make it hard to recommend. There are better multiplayer games out there that respect your time more. 

Gameplay: 4/10 Bad | Overall: 3/10 Very Bad

This game is great for playing with friends but if you choose to play solo the game will pair you up with either really great randoms or players that sabotage the entire team and never get punished for it. Secondly, there is a massive toxicity issue with both survivors and killers that ruin gameplay and can damage your mental health

For example, you can be t-bagged by your own team mates, some killers may specifically tunnel you out because of the character you choose to play, I've had someone target me because of having my art tiktok in my bio, there are survivors that aim to eliminate other survivors and work with the killer. The end game chat is often filled with hate and discrimination after a match. and these examples don't even cover the amount of toxicity in the game

The reason toxicity is so common in  this game is because they know they can get away with it; the reporting system isn't clear on whether or not players get punished for their behaviour and the only if you take the report further though email, it will only be looked at weeks/months later due to the high amount of reports they have

So no, I don't recommend unless you like to be harassed, bullied and berated by the sheer amount of toxic players that dominate this game

Matchmaking is horrible, survivor perks are completely unbalanced making the game unbearable for most killers. the strongest killers are also unbalanced. map design looks cool but the oversaturation of pallets, and windows that connect into themselves make disgusting loops that most killers can't deal with, and this is singlehandedly one of the most toxic playerbases in gaming, especially on the survivor players side. they made up a whole rulebook about how killers should play, except when killers actually do follow that rulebook they just lose because all it does is prevent them from using strategies that actually win games. i'm beyond sick of how survivor players will keep bringing busted builds, bully killers, and tell them to kill themselves if they win, that the devs refuse to fix because survivors are their cash cows while simultaneously ignoring the killer player base. the game is in a state, and has been for quite some time where playing killer feels absolutely awful. too many bugs, to toxic of a player base, ignorant devs, horrible map and perk balancing, not fun to play anymore. i would not suggest anyone buy this game.

One of the hardest games to recommend I've ever played. These hours on my account don't accurately represent the other hundreds of hours I have on other platforms. I've been playing DbD for 6 years, and its the only reason I can even still play, because I've expended considerable effort and watched twenty hours of Otzdarva videos making me semi-decent at best at this game.

If you just stumbled onto this on steam and don't know the first thing about DbD, you likely will not enjoy it.
If you don't want to watch hours of youtube videos explaining why the killer found you hiding in a corner out of sight, or why you can never seem to catch up to a survivor, you likely will not enjoy it.
If you don't have money for or don't want to spend money on characters to gain access to the most basic abilities everyone else is using, you likely will not enjoy it.
If you (very reasonably) can't handle the idea of being bad at something for reasons that you can't begin to understand, you likely will not enjoy it.

Dead by Daylight is an asymmetrical multiplayer game unlike anything else that ever came before it, because its biggest fight isn't between the hatchet-wielding maniac and his potential victims, it's between the people who have been playing for ten years and the person who saw a video of Springtrap chasing Trevor Belmont and thought it might be fun. There is nothing, and I mean nothing, that this game can or will do to make a new player understand why they won't be good or even passable at the game for many, many hours. You cannot learn the true basics of the game on your own, you WILL have to have someone explain it, and if you want to have a casual experience where you don't care about winning or losing, you are almost guaranteed to be ruining the experience for someone who does, or vice versa.

I personally think the game should be free to start. 12 characters sounds like a lot, but once you realize that their perks are passable at best and miles from what you will see 70% of other players using, the game asserts the need to spend either a considerable amount of time, or your hard-earned, honest-to-God dollars to get your hands on the rest of the game. Not just that, but I shudder to imagine how much of the money Behaviour has in their pocket from people who bought this thinking the idea sounded enjoyable, and by the time the refund period was over, they realized it was an unapproachable mess.

If you've read all that, and still think you might want to try starting it; if you like watching youtube videos and spending money on characters you don't want to play as; if you want to join the internet's worst abusive relationship, then take my hand, and join the club. The wins are tight, The losses are heartbreaking. The clutches are hype. The moments of just barely getting away from the killer and breaking chase will have you shouting for joy. 

I'm having fun, but Christ did I have to work for it.
Toxic community and bugs ruin an otherwise unique and compelling experience.
devs just twisting their asshair instead of improving this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game and its servers dont bother buying only if u want misery and hell

This game is the most broken thing I have ever played. Trying to reach Killer grade iri 1 and once I reach iri3 with 3 points my game freezes as I'm about to get the 4k, a few moments later the game kicks me to the lobby and says connection error and that I was "AFK". This ADDITIONALLY removes FOUR of my rank up points... Neither of these reasons for disconnection can be true as I am in a discord call with my friends having 0 connectivity issues and perfect ping, and I was most definately NOT afk. So I moved on and waited the 5 minute ban... Until 2 games later the SAME ISSUE OCCURS WHEN I PICK UP A SURVIVOR, only this time, I lose another 4 points and get reset to IRIDESCENT 4, with now a FIFTEEN MINUTE BAN?!?!? So I reload the game and verify the integrity of my game files, to which no issue comes up, as predicted. But just to put the cherry on top, when I load back into the game, my Killer grade has been deranked ONCE AGAIN, but not by 4 points this time, no, instead to GOLD ONE AN ENTIRE RANK DOWN. Fix your game behaviour you bunch of morons.

Most of the comminity is horrificaly toxic and it is so unbalanced to the point where if you see specific killers, you see people just DC or give up. The amount of hours that people accumulate on this game are conserning. Despite this, I get it. It is a genuinely fun game when people arent running the meta and some people in the comunity are so sweet and funny. I would recomend this game if you have a lot of time on your hands. Just dont play it if you crash out easily.

Do I recommend this game? Yes and no.

The core game itself is fun as hell. However the player base (as with most games) and learning curve makes this game hard to recommend to new people. 

DBD is NOT beginner friendly. SBMM doesn't actually exist. You are going to suffer, a lot. This game punishes any tiny little itty bitty mistake like your actual life depends on it. A SINGLE misstep can lose ENTIRE game. You are basically getting thrown into a pit of lava and told GOOD LUCK. The learning curve itself is more of a cliff you have to figure out how to climb up barefoot and naked. "Meta" perks are behind paywalls. Most other perks are completely useless. But you're going to have to memorize a lot of them to be marginally successful.

You go up against a roster of 43 killers with their own "unique" powers, perks, builds and addons... You never know what you'll get, or you'll get the same killer 2-3 times in a row. Oh, and if you are new, they will know, and they make it their life mission to ruin your entire day. BMing, hard tunneling, humping you on the ground, the works. 

If you want to play killer? Congratulations, you're going up against a pool of 52 different survivors that all have their own perks. Just like playing survivor it's a complete gamble as to what you'll get. SWF? Bully squad? Gen rushers? Meme squad? Roll the dice and hope you get a decent game. God forbid you accidentally (or intentionally) tunnel or slug because you'll get death threats in endgame chat. Or if you decide to not be friendly. Meanwhile the survivors are actively t-bagging, clicking their flashlights at you, and taunting you and you are not allowed to be mad about this. Because only THEY are allowed to do that of course :) 

DBD has one of the whiniest "woe is me it's everyone else's fault I lost" communities I've ever seen. Too many people that play this game get BIG mad if you don't follow the arbitrary made up rules set by the community (not by the game creators btw). You will also end up getting hate for playing certain survivors or killers (♥♥♥♥ ghoul remove him from the game I hate all of you). 

Don't forget the micro aggression if you happen to play any POC survivor!!!!!!! Or if you use any of the pride charms. Or even actual aggression and straight up ABUSE for playing Orela, David, or Tubarão, and the perpetrator will go completely unpunished because BHVR's report system is more of a joke than using tissue paper as a bullet proof vest :D

If you're still reading and wondering what is actually good about the game... Well, those matches where you play well, your team synergy is on point, get good loops in and end up with a 3-4 man out will give you an adrenaline high that is unlike any other. Not to mention the odd funny killer or survivor game. Those types of games are what keeps me addicted, and I keep crawling back every damn time.
The reason I need therapy but still choose to spend my money on this rather than therapy itself.

The killers side of this is not any different but what gets even worse is that no one on the dev team likes to play killer. so if you want to be a killer you are actively fighting against the survivors AND the devs. if you find a good way to play killer you better believe it will be nerfed in the very next patch. The second any survivor player thinks you played poorly or unsportsmanlike you will be reported and badgered constantly and best believe that if a survivor is being unsportsmanlike they will taunt you relentlessly and call you a horrible killer. On the survivor side the game is boring beyond belief. No wonder the most joy survivor players get out of this game is ragebaiting killers because if it were any other way this game would be a QTE simulator. You used to sneak around killers to get objectives done but its more or less just running in a circle around a small part of the map at this point. Since there are so many survivor mains that have learned that this is how the devs WANT the game to be like this its resulted in 1 person doing whats called a "loop" around the killer and the other 3 doing QTEs all game long or its 2 people doing QTEs and the other person is actually just following the killer while the killer is chasing the looper. So that just incase the killer does finally catch up or gets a very very lucky hit on the survivor the second survivor is very close by to either:
1. body block the killer to stall for the carried survivor to wiggle out
2.drop a flashbang at the killers feet to stun them and drop the survivor
3. shine a flashlight at the killer so they get stunned
4. break hooks before a killer can get to it.
or
5. bait the killer into wasting time by swinging at them

For many years, I never thought I'd be much of a fan of Dead by Daylight. I've never been much of a horror game fan and have always been more of an MMO RPG and FPS enjoyer.

This year, my friends asked me to play the game with them since I had it available through my Steam family sharing library. We played a few games, and those few games quickly turned into many. I ended up enjoying the game so much that I now feel somewhat addicted to it, and I've since purchased the game for myself along with multiple DLCs and in-game cosmetics.

The gameplay is incredibly fun and exciting, especially when playing with friends and teaming up against the killers. It doesn't feel overwhelmingly scary like many horror games tend to be, but the thrill of being chased by the killer, or sneaking around not knowing when they're going to appear, still gives you a rush. I also love the fact that there are tons of different abilities (perks) that let you customize how you want to play. You can create builds that allow you to face killers head-on, or focus on stealth and completing objectives as quickly as possible while avoiding chases altogether.

As for playing as the killer, I unfortunately can't say too much since I haven't spent nearly as much time in that role. I've mostly enjoyed the game from the survivor perspective. However, from the little time I have spent playing killer, I can say that instead of giving you the rush of feeling like the prey, it gives you the more intense experience of being the hunter. Tracking down survivors one by one and preventing them from completing their objectives can be stressful, but it never feels forced or unenjoyable.

To sum it up, as someone who never thought they would enjoy this game, I think giving Dead by Daylight a chance is definitely worth it, especially if you have friends to play with, as that makes the experience even more enjoyable.

10/10

You would only enjoy this game if you are playing with friends (aka SWF). Killers are constantly being nerfed to the point where only streamer-level players can keep up with survivors. Survivors are simply TOO STRONG. After a certain number of hours, the horror element completely vanishes because killers no longer pose a threat.

In fact, when you start out as a killer, you will enjoy it. However, as your MMR rises over time, there is no way to compete with experienced survivors anymore. A single mistake will cost you the entire match, leading to a guaranteed loss (3-4 escapes). Having owned this game for several years, I no longer feel any mystery or suspense as a survivor, simply because killers rarely win at high MMR—which defeats the whole purpose of the killer role.

In addition, the matchmaking system is ridiculous. As a player with 600 hours, I constantly get matched against survivors with 2,000 to 3,000 hours. I see absolutely no logic behind this.

RECOMMENDED if you have friends who are highly experienced and you want to enjoy trolling the killer together.
NOT RECOMMENDED if you are a solo queue player or want to main the killer role.

It is so fun, I play with friends on most nights. It does take a good amount of skill and strategy which is what I like in games. You also have level progression which means you can see how much you improve within time. I would 100% recommend this game.

If they had any talent, they'd be able to pump out content left and right and let you unlock at least 50% of it while locking down the other 50% for profit, but they just can't do that. I hate this game so much. I despise it so much that I would rather jump into a volcano filled with fiber glass than to play as killer in this game again. this game is genuinely designed in every way to be unfun. they release a new killer after a new killer, more powerful than the last, allowing people who are even marginally good to win almost every time, which, with this design, makes survivor, the characters that are in a higher necessity due to the simple rule of 1v4, actively less fun to play, making killer less fun to play as it takes longer to queue. Not to mention this game has worse microtransactions than destiny and fortnite combined, while also being a PAID GAME ITSELF. it is a MIRACLE this game has even stayed relevant this long.
edit: after playing killer aswell, this game is still, just as digustingly unfun to play without other people, and even then its a 50/50. all you will get is digusting sweats who's only purpose is to seal club and make sure you do not have fun.
game has something to do with looping and pallets or vaults idk but i enjoy having a miserable time playing this

DBD is great! love the work, BHVR. Playerbase is pretty good, graphics are great, lore is pretty good and surprisingly easy to understand. Game runs great, and I'm playing on a Gaming Laptop. Most of the killers are great and fun to play with/against. It requires actual skill to play the game unlike some Aysm Horrors.
I have played 6 7 hours and love the game. Super unique and fun to play with friendly neighbours. MUST GET ABSOLUTELY.

Honestly it took me taking a fourth or fifth MAJOR break from this game to realize just how toxic of a relationship I have with it. I have a lot of friends in the community of this game, and a lot of good memories with it. But every patch that drops is me hoping it gets better, and every patch i walk away knowing it probably never will. 2v8 is an especially bad time to play, the 1v4 queue is insufferable and the 2v8 queue is 30 minutes long. Flashlights are annoying as hell and there's STILL not an option to change what the flash looks like even though it irritates the hell out of my eyes. I'm probably going to keep buying dlcs, because for some reason I can't drop this game no matter how hard I try. But with my stints with the game getting shorter and shorter, I can't even be sure of that. 
Bugs people call "techs," annoying survivors that sit in the gates in endgame to waste time, annoying killers that hold you hostage because your friend dared to want to escape via the hatch. The game wastes your time as much as some players seem keen to. When you play with friends, it can be enjoyable. The game is at its best when it feels like a horror movie, when people run at the sight of the killer. But when survivors run in your way to body block or to flashlight save every pickup it ruins the immersion of that, and the gameplay on its own, while very in depth with builds and whatnot, is too shallow to really feel good beyond that.

If you think you don't like this game, I wouldn't give it a second chance. If you let it sink its teeth in, it's hard to let it go.

Dead by Daylight somehow managed to make 2v8 feel less fun with this patch. My first match of the night ends in a DC and I instantly get hit with a matchmaking lock... in a limited-time arcade-style mode that nobody treats competitively to begin with.

Meanwhile the new patch added this bugged fog effect that tears across the screen during matches, which somehow became more noticeable than the actual gameplay improvements.

And somehow we still don’t have an option to mute or disable menu character animations/audio, so certain killers are constantly grunting, screaming, or making noise in the background while you sit in queue.

Feels like BHVR is more focused on punishing players than fixing obvious issues.

as  a relatively new dbd player, i would say this game is NOT beginner friendly at all, there's a lot to unpack and its not the type of game u figure the flow of it easily on your own, specially if you run into swf's constantly, you need to put in mind if you got the game to play as killer your gonna put in twice the effort to learn killer than survivor, but later on, the game will become super easy for you, but if your getting it to be survivor, then you will get the hang of it relatively fast (longer than average though) but your gonna struggle on the long run.

HELP.

The devs have systematically made survivor impossible to lose unless you literally have less than 2 braincells. The community will shove these "unwritten rules" that basically amount to "no don't punish my bad choices". The devs are literally gentle parenting survivors giving them fallback after fallback and then we're "surprised" when survivor players are entitled and killer players are miserable and only use the most broken addon/perk combinations. Every once in a while you'll get a genuinely fun game and if you come at this game with a less serious group of friends to play survivor with or you have to get a whole 5 man for some private matches to enjoy. I genuinely hate writing this review because I've sank years of my life and met so many great friends from this game. I know you're reading this looking at my ~150 hours on steam and calling bs but I've bought this game on 2 different playstation accounts and steam, I just play the least on steam.

Rest in peace Puppers I hope you're comfy.

I have been playing this game for over 200 hours, and can confidently say that I can see why this game gets such a bad rep, and for only two reasons. 1; The matchmaking. If you're expecting a chill horror game session with friends, FIND A DIFFERENT GAME. More likely than not, you're going to go against a p50 or 100 killer/survivor team, right off the start. Which is like getting thrown into fire, that's on fire, THAT'S ON FIRE. And 2; You will be bullied. It does not matter if you're new, if you have no idea what's going on, or it's your first match ever. You will be told to die, you will be called slurs, and you will be mocked both in game and out of game no matter your level. My advice? Just don't. I wish I never played this game. The game itself is fun and entertaining, with many appeals. But the community? Absolute garbage. Good luck to those who choose not to heed my warning. You shall find out soon enough.
